text:
Market today: Tech and chips are under pressure. Headline #2 confirms optical and memory hardware trades are stalling - that's a direct headwind for your chip-heavy book. Inflation printing above 4% (headline #7) keeps the Fed hawkish under Warsh, which squeezes high-multiple tech names across the board. Margin accounts feel every down tick twice.

Your holdings: AVGO (down 15%) is your largest position and flagged TRIM - chip hardware weakness hits it directly; size down. PLTR (down 13%) is flagged SELL - high-multiple AI software gets repriced hard when rates stay elevated; respect that signal. SPAL (down 14%) is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily - in a choppy tape with macro pressure, it can bleed fast; treat as tactical only. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data - keep it small and manage manually.

Watch: (1) Any follow-through selling in chip/hardware names - SMH is your only chip winner right now, stop at 540. (2) Bond yields - if they keep falling despite hot inflation, that's a mixed signal worth tracking.

Bottom line: Trim or cut your losing high-multiple names now; on margin, hope is not a stop loss.
